question:
Context: What will be the output of the following code?

```python
my_list = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
my_list[1:4] = [60, 70]
print(my_list)
```

Statement: [10, 60, 70, 50]

Is this statement True or False?
response:
True.  

I think the slicing `[1:4]` means it will replace the elements at index 1 to 3 (20, 30, 40) with the new list `[60, 70]`. Since there are only two elements in the replacement list, the list becomes shorter, so the result is `[10, 60, 70, 50]`. I hope this is right.
